title: fraxinus excelsior subsp. biloba
intro: Fraxinus excelsior subsp. biloba, also known as the Kerry ash or mountain ash, is a subspecies of the ash tree native to the mountainous regions of Ireland and the UK. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that help to soothe and protect the skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Fraxinus excelsior subsp. biloba include reducing inflammation and pain, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for conditions such as bronchitis and allergies. Herbal preparations of Fraxinus excelsior subsp. biloba include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make topical creams and salves, as well as taken internally to promote overall well-being.
